title: Support specifying user networks when creating pods
body:
This is my attempt at implementing the wiring to support #4718 as-is.

This --network argument is slightly different than the --net/--network argument accepted when creating containers. I'm sure there's also other inconsistencies and such. If this general direction is welcomed then I'll revise as necessary; however I haven't managed to run the tests yet to know how well my test works. If waiting for a proper refactor as discussed in #4718 is preferred then ignore this PR i guess.

comments.body:
Hi @danopia. Thanks for your PR.

I'm waiting for a [containers](https://github.com/orgs/containers/people) member to verify that this patch is reasonable to test. If it is, they should reply with `/ok-to-test` on its own line. Until that is done, I will not automatically test new commits in this PR, but the usual testing commands by org members will still work. Regular contributors should [join the org](https://github.com/orgs/containers/people) to skip this step.

Once the patch is verified, the new status will be reflected by the `ok-to-test` label.

I understand the commands that are listed [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod).

<details>

Instructions for interacting with me using PR comments are available [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/pull-requests.md).  If you have questions or suggestions related to my behavior, please file an issue against the [kubernetes/test-infra](https://github.com/kubernetes/test-infra/issues/new?title=Prow%20issue:) repository.
</details>

Thanks @danopia.  First thing you need to do is sign your PR.

git commit -a --amend -s
git push --force

Should fix this, and then the tests should run.

/approve

Need @mheon and @haircommander to review, I know they had some thoughts on redoing the podman pod create stuff.

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *danopia*, *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

thanks @danopia the code LGTM

more philosophically, it feels a bit wasteful to me to add patches to a code base that is destined to fundamentally change. However, I can't commit any time to making that change, and I appreciate the time you've put in here, so this feeling is non-blocking
A friendly reminder that this PR had no activity for 30 days.
@danopia Are you still able to work on this?
If not, I'll take this over - I'd love to have this in the next release

Hello, I'm dealing with some life events recently, and don't expect to continue pushing for this merge myself. I used bare containers with shared networks for my own needs and it's good enough for now.

I'd love to see this feature happen still, so I can start using proper pods at some point. I'll close this PR in the meantime. Looking forward to it @mheon
